claims_verified.claim: --advisory --no-input dry-run preview now carries the waived judgment-gate findings instead of dropping them
claims_verified.evidence: local_merge.py:441-467: the dry-run swap now branches on verdict.advisory, building distinct reasons/digest naming the waived findings, and preserves advisory=verdict.advisory on the rebuilt MergeVerdict. Reproduced directly: LocalMergeEngine(dry_run=True, advisory_mode=True) on an L1 branch with a security-panel blocker now returns kind='dry_run', reasons=('would merge under --advisory ... judgment gates WOULD be waived:', 'security panel blocker(s): IDOR'), advisory=('security panel blocker(s): IDOR',).

claims_verified.claim: CLI report distinguishes the advisory dry-run preview from a clean one
claims_verified.evidence: local_merge.py:1117-1127: DRY_RUN branch now prints '[dry-run*] ... WOULD be WAIVED ...' and points to merge-hold.md when v.advisory is set, vs the plain '[dry-run] ... WOULD auto-merge' otherwise.

claims_verified.claim: New tests cover the previously-missing combination
claims_verified.evidence: tests/test_local_merge.py:408 test_engine_dry_run_advisory_preview_carries_waived_findings, :434 test_engine_dry_run_clean_branch_keeps_the_plain_preview, and a CLI-level test_cli_advisory_dry_run_preview_is_honest_and_mutates_nothing at :1020; full suite tests/test_local_merge.py + tests/test_trust_blockers.py: 120 passed.
ruff check: All checks passed; basedpyright: 0 errors, 84 warnings (pre-existing, non-blocking).

test_assessment: The previously-missing dry-run+advisory interaction is now directly covered at both the engine level (advisory-present and advisory-absent cases) and the CLI level (asserting the preview mutates nothing and names the waived findings honestly).
residual_risks: --advisory + --local combination remains untested, as previously noted; no concrete failure identified.
_record_advisory's git commit on a byte-identical re-run remains a theoretical, previously-noted residual risk, unrelated to this finding.
